name: Author continuation accepts durable force source with owned procedure budget

source.purpose: Regression coverage for Ch2 Author setup failures where an arc durable force is used as the antagonist source and procedural surfaces are already grounded by human_stakes plus continuation_dramatic_turn.procedure_budget.

turns.playerInput: I clear the hold and take the route chit.
turns.narratorProse: The ship leaves the berth with the route chit authenticated and the beacon corridor ahead.

arcPlan.title: Forty Thousand: Running Lane

sourceHook.title: Forty Thousand
sourceHook.premise: The crew must move sealed cargo through contested beacon windows.
sourceHook.objective: Thread the corridor without becoming owned.
sourceHook.crucible: Every clean clearance creates a sharper future claim.
sourceHook.firstEpisode: Leave the berth and enter the beacon corridor.

scenarioShape.premise: The crew runs sealed cargo through a collapsing beacon corridor.
scenarioShape.whyThisRun: The route itself is the trap.

arcPlan.arcQuestion: Can the crew thread the corridor without becoming owned?
arcPlan.coreCrucible: The corridor can free the crew only by exposing them to new ownership.
invariantFacts: The crew carries sealed cargo through contested beacon windows.
variableTruthsForThisRun: Who priced the route chit before departure.

durableForces.name: Kessler Span Fleet
durableForces.agenda: Tax every ship crossing the middle beacon window.
durableForces.leverage: Controls the toll lane and route authorization.
durableForces.fear: A visible bypass makes the toll soft.
durableForces.pressureStyle: Casual toll demands that become exact under refusal.

pressureEngines.name: Beacon Window Decay
pressureEngines.advancesWhen: The crew delays at corridor nodes.
pressureEngines.slowsWhen: The crew moves decisively through a window.
pressureEngines.visibleSymptoms: Route countdowns and beacon flicker warnings.

meaning.situation: The PC converted the berth hold into a corridor run.
meaning.tension: Freedom from the berth became exposure to route owners.
meaning.ticking: The route chit stayed active as passage authorization.
meaning.question: Who controls the route after the ship leaves the berth?
meaning.closer: The first beacon blinks ahead as Kessler traffic wakes.

transitionSeed.priorChapterMeant: The PC bought motion by accepting route terms.
transitionSeed.earnedConsequence: Kessler can price the next window before the crew arrives.
transitionSeed.worsenedDetail: The route chit names the corridor passage authorization.
transitionSeed.unresolvedQuestion: Who controls the route after the berth falls behind?

chapter_frame.title: Kessler Prices Passage
chapter_frame.premise: Kessler Span Fleet turns the authenticated route chit into a toll claim before the crew reaches the middle beacon.
chapter_frame.active_pressure: The toll captain can honor passage or make the crew visible to every lane hunter.
chapter_frame.central_tension: Pay Kessler's price, expose the cargo, or make the crew a public bypass example.
chapter_frame.objective: Cross Kessler's toll window without handing over cargo leverage or crew standing.
chapter_frame.crucible: The clean route chit now lets Kessler decide what clean passage costs.

outcome_spectrum.clean: Raffe still trusts the route plan, and Kessler loses the cleanest toll claim.
outcome_spectrum.costly: Maren lets the crew through, but the PC owes a favor.
outcome_spectrum.failure: Kessler exposes the cargo signature and hunters know the ship.
outcome_spectrum.catastrophic: Crew loyalty breaks as Kessler owns the next route choice.

opening_scene_spec.location: Driftrunner helm at the corridor edge
opening_scene_spec.initial_state: The first beacon is behind the ship, and Kessler traffic wakes ahead of the toll window.
opening_scene_spec.first_player_facing: Captain Maren offers to honor the route_chit if the PC lets her inspect one cargo seal.
opening_scene_spec.dramatic_situation: Kessler converts the prior clearance into a public toll demand with crew trust watching.
opening_scene_spec.first_visible_pressure: Maren can make passage look lawful or make refusal look like smuggling.
opening_scene_spec.first_human_or_institutional_move: Maren names Raffe on open comms before asking for the seal.
withheld_premise_facts: Maren already knows the route chit was prepriced.

antagonist_field.core_pressure: Kessler prices passage through social exposure before violence.

default_face.name: Captain Maren
default_face.pressure_style: friendly toll language sharpened by public names

possible_faces.name: Captain Maren
possible_faces.becomes_primary_when: The PC refuses inspection or names a rival protection claim.
possible_faces.pressure_style: friendly toll language sharpened by public names

antagonist_field.escalation_logic: Each refusal lets Maren trade courtesy for exposure.

starting_npcs.name: Captain Maren
starting_npcs.disposition_reason: toll captains distrust crews using prepriced passage

starting_npcs.name: Raffe
starting_npcs.hidden_pressure: Will remember if the PC hides terms.
starting_npcs.disposition_reason: they have backed the PC through the prior exit

starting_npcs.name: Seln Varo
starting_npcs.disposition_reason: signal officers treat unknown cargo as leverage

active_threads.title: The route ownership
active_threads.question: Who controls the route now?
active_threads.resolution_criteria: The route owner is accepted, bypassed, or exposed.
active_threads.failure_mode: The crew crosses only on someone else's terms.

active_threads.title: Kessler's toll price
active_threads.question: What does passage cost?
active_threads.resolution_criteria: The toll is paid, evaded, renegotiated, or turned against Maren.
active_threads.failure_mode: Kessler records the crew as toll property.

active_threads.title: Cargo exposure
active_threads.question: Can the seal stay private?
active_threads.resolution_criteria: The cargo seal is protected, traded, or exposed.
active_threads.failure_mode: The cargo signature becomes public leverage.

active_threads.title: Crew trust
active_threads.question: Does Raffe still back the route?
active_threads.resolution_criteria: The PC shares enough truth for Raffe to stay aligned.
active_threads.failure_mode: Raffe treats the route plan as another hidden debt.

arc_link.chapter_function: Make the first corridor toll cost crew trust or cargo exposure.
arc_link.player_stance_read: Watch whether the PC protects speed, secrecy, or crew consent.

pacing_contract.chapter_question: Can the crew cross without giving Kessler ownership?
pacing_contract.early_pressure: Maren makes the route chit a public toll claim.

continuation_moves.prior_chapter_meaning: The PC left the berth by accepting route terms.
continuation_moves.larger_pattern_revealed: Route authorities become people who can price crew loyalty.

new_named_threat_from_prior_success.name: Captain Maren
new_named_threat_from_prior_success.why_inevitable: Kessler controls the next toll window

worsened_existing_thread.why_load_bearing_now: authentication lets Kessler prove the crew needs passage

planted_midchapter_revelation.hidden_statement: Maren knew the toll price before departure.
planted_midchapter_revelation.recontextualizes: The route chit was bait, not simple passage.

relationship_deepening_target.pressure: Raffe needs truth before backing another hidden term.

continuation_dramatic_turn.prior_chapter_meant: The PC traded station freedom for corridor exposure.
continuation_dramatic_turn.larger_pattern_revealed: The next route owner can price loyalty before firing a shot.

pressure_owner.why_they_now_act: Kessler controls the next toll window.

human_leverage.what_they_can_take_or_offer: Maren can offer passage or expose the cargo signature.
human_leverage.what_they_need_from_pc: She needs the PC to accept Kessler's price publicly.

worsened_detail.why_it_is_load_bearing_now: It proves the crew is already inside Kessler's toll lane.

continuation_dramatic_turn.offscreen_antagonist_presence: Vantabloc is absent; Kessler makes its clearance look sellable.

procedure_budget.dramatic_function: Maren uses authorization to make passage a loyalty test.

pressure_ladder.trigger_condition: Maren rebroadcasts the toll demand under the PC's name.
The PC refuses inspection without offering another proof.
The crew keeps moving after Maren's final toll demand.

human_stakes.what_is_lost: Raffe stops treating the route plan as a shared risk.
The PC becomes known as a crew leader Kessler can name publicly.

possible_revelations.statement: The route authorization was prepriced before departure.
possible_revelations.recontextualizes: The toll was waiting before the crew reached Kessler.

possible_revelations.statement: Maren needs the PC's public yes more than the toll.
possible_revelations.emergence_condition: Maren is pressed on why she names Raffe.
possible_revelations.recontextualizes: The toll demand is a loyalty trap.

moral_fault_lines.why_it_hurts: Speed protects cargo while secrecy spends trust.
Either makes someone own the route.

editorialized_lore.item: Kessler tolls are performed in public.

editorialized_lore.relevance_now: The passage proof now costs loyalty.
